name: PNC-27
subtitle: Anti-Cancer Peptide | p53-HDM-2 Disruptor

molecular.halfLife: Less than 24 hours (requires daily dosing in studies)

overview: PNC-27 is an experimental anti-cancer peptide created by a supercomputer at SUNY Downstate Medical Center in 2000. It contains an HDM-2 binding domain from p53 (residues 12-26) linked to a cell-penetrating domain. The peptide selectively kills cancer cells by binding to HDM-2 (MDM2) expressed on cancer cell membranes, forming pores that cause cell necrosis. Critically, PNC-27 has no effect on normal cells because healthy cells don't express HDM-2 on their membranes. Research shows effectiveness against pancreatic cancer, breast cancer, leukemia, and melanoma.
mechanism: PNC-27 exploits a unique vulnerability of cancer cells: the presence of HDM-2 (human double minute 2, also called MDM2) on their cell surface. The peptide's p53 residues adopt a conformation that binds directly to membrane-bound HDM-2, inducing transmembrane pore formation. This causes rapid tumor cell necrosis (not apoptosis). Additionally, PNC-27 enters cancer cells and disrupts mitochondrial membranes. Normal cells lack surface HDM-2 expression and are completely spared.
keyBenefits: No effect on normal healthy cells
Does not require p53 function in target cells

items.name: Pancreatic Cancer
items.description: PNC-27 shows selective cytotoxicity against pancreatic cancer cells in research.

items.name: Breast Cancer
items.description: Demonstrated effectiveness against breast cancer cell lines.

items.name: Leukemia
items.description: Induces necrosis of K-562 leukemia cells through HDM-2 binding.

items.name: Melanoma
items.description: Shows selective targeting of melanoma cells.

items.name: HDM-2 Expressing Tumors
items.description: Most effective against cancers with high membrane HDM-2 expression.

items.name: Tumor Selectivity Research
items.description: Model compound for studying cancer-selective therapies.

deliveryMethods.overview: PNC-27 is currently in preclinical research stages. All dosing data comes from in vitro and animal model studies. No human dosing protocols have been approved. The peptide has shown tumor eradication in nude mice xenograft models.

interactions.notes: Potential for combination therapy being researched.

timeline.effects: Pore formation and cancer cell necrosis
Human clinical trials not yet conducted

stopSigns: Not applicable - not approved for human use
contraindications: Cancers without membrane HDM-2 may not respond

warning: Not all cancers express membrane HDM-2

references.title: Anticancer peptide PNC-27 adopts an HDM-2-binding conformation and kills cancer cells by binding to HDM-2 in their membranes
references.keyFindings: PNC-27 binds membrane-bound HDM-2, inducing pore formation and cancer cell necrosis while sparing normal cells.

references.title: Anti-Cancer Peptide PNC-27 Kills Cancer Cells by Unique Interactions with Plasma Membrane-Bound hdm-2 and with Mitochondrial Membranes
references.keyFindings: PNC-27 also enters cancer cells and disrupts mitochondrial membranes.

references.title: PNC-27 Induces Tumor Cell Necrosis of Leukemia Cells
references.keyFindings: PNC-27 induces necrosis of K-562 leukemia cells lacking p53 through p53-independent pathway.

references.title: PNC-27, a Chimeric p53-Penetratin Peptide
references.keyFindings: PNC-27 binds HDM-2 in p53 peptide-like structure, induces selective membrane-pore formation leading to cancer cell lysis.

quickStats.cycleDuration: Not established - no human clinical trials
